title: STEM Summer in America
intro: A national directory of STEM summer programs, camps, and research institutes students can attend across the USA. Filter by focus, location, and cost. Deadlines and dates change every year — always confirm details on the program's official site before applying.
disclaimer: Skynet Nexus lists programs for discovery only. We are not affiliated with these organizations. Verify dates, ages, eligibility, tuition, and application deadlines on each program's official website.

programs.name: MIT Beaver Works Summer Institute
programs.summary: A rigorous, project-based STEM program in AI, autonomous systems, and engineering. Students build real hardware and software over an intense residential month. Free to admitted students.

programs.name: Research Science Institute (RSI)
programs.org: Center for Excellence in Education & MIT
programs.summary: One of the most selective STEM research programs in the world. Students complete original, mentored research and present at a symposium. Fully funded, including room and board.

programs.name: MITES Summer
programs.org: MIT Introduction to Technology, Engineering & Science
programs.summary: A no-cost residential program for high-achieving students from underrepresented or underserved backgrounds, focused on science and engineering rigor and college readiness.

programs.name: Summer Science Program (SSP)
programs.location: Multiple university campuses (CA, NM, IN, CO)
programs.summary: Residential research in astrophysics, biochemistry, genomics, or synthetic chemistry. Teams do real science — like computing the orbit of a near-Earth asteroid — with faculty mentors.

programs.name: NASA SEES Summer Intern Program
programs.org: NASA / UT Austin Center for Space Research
programs.summary: A NASA-sponsored internship where students analyze real Earth and space science data alongside scientists. Nationally competitive and fully funded for selected interns.

programs.name: Space Camp
programs.summary: The iconic astronaut-training experience: simulated missions, rocket builds, and teamwork challenges. Programs scale from younger campers up to aviation and robotics academies.

programs.name: National Flight Academy
programs.summary: An immersive aviation and STEM adventure aboard a simulated aircraft carrier, blending flight simulation, physics, and teamwork into a week-long deployment.

programs.name: iD Tech Camps
programs.summary: The largest STEM camp network in the country: coding, game development, AI, robotics, and design, held on major university campuses and online with small class sizes.

programs.name: Johns Hopkins CTY Summer Programs
programs.summary: Accelerated academic courses in cryptology, robotics, data science, engineering, and more, for motivated learners from elementary through high school.

programs.name: CyberStart America
programs.org: SANS Institute & National Cyber Scholarship Foundation
programs.summary: A free, gamified cybersecurity program where students crack codes and solve security challenges, with a path to national scholarships. No experience required.

programs.name: GenCyber Summer Camps
programs.org: National Security Agency & National Science Foundation
programs.summary: Free cybersecurity camps hosted at colleges across the country, funded by the NSA and NSF, teaching safe online practices and the fundamentals of cyber defense.

programs.name: Girls Who Code Summer Programs
programs.summary: Free virtual programs introducing girls and nonbinary students to computer science, game design, and web development, with industry mentors and a supportive community.

programs.name: AI4ALL Summer Programs
programs.summary: University-hosted programs that open up artificial intelligence to students from underrepresented groups, pairing hands-on AI projects with ethics and mentorship.

programs.name: Girlstart Summer Camp
programs.summary: Hands-on STEM day camps designed to keep elementary and middle-school girls excited about science, engineering, and math through experiments and design challenges.

programs.name: PROMYS
programs.org: Program in Mathematics for Young Scientists — Boston University
programs.summary: A six-week immersion in deep mathematical problem-solving and number theory, where students discover advanced ideas for themselves alongside mathematician mentors.

programs.name: Ross Mathematics Program
programs.summary: An intensive summer of abstract mathematics built around the motto 'think deeply of simple things,' guiding students to build number theory from the ground up.

programs.name: COSMOS
programs.org: California State Summer School for Mathematics & Science
programs.summary: A four-week residential program on UC campuses where students dive into university-level clusters spanning robotics, biotechnology, astrophysics, and more.

programs.name: Simons Summer Research Program
programs.summary: Students join a university research group as apprentices, work on hands-on projects with faculty mentors, and present their findings — with a stipend for participants.

programs.name: Garcia Summer Research Program
programs.summary: A seven-week materials science and polymer research program where students learn to conduct original, publishable research in engineering and the life sciences.

programs.name: Anson L. Clark Scholars Program
programs.summary: A highly selective, seven-week research program pairing a small cohort with faculty mentors across science, engineering, and the humanities — with a stipend included.

programs.name: Michigan Math and Science Scholars
programs.summary: Two-week university courses taught by U-M faculty on frontier topics from cryptography and forensic physics to mathematics of decisions, elections, and the brain.

programs.name: National Youth Science Camp
programs.summary: An all-expenses-paid honor for two graduating seniors from each state: lectures from leading scientists, hands-on studies, and outdoor adventure in the mountains.

programs.name: Governor's Schools (STEM)
programs.summary: State-sponsored summer programs — many free — offering advanced STEM study for talented students. Find your state's program through the national conference directory.

programs.name: Black Girls Code Summer Programs
programs.summary: Workshops and summer camps introducing Black girls and youth of color to coding, web design, robotics, and game development in a welcoming, community-driven space.
